2606.25552.title: SFL-MTSC: Leveraging Semantic Frame-Level Multi-Task Self-Consistency for Robust Multi-Intent Spoken Language Understanding
2606.25552.paper_summary: Prompt-based spoken language understanding (SLU) with large language models (LLMs) often suffers from inconsistent intent--slot structures due to decoding stochasticity, particularly in multi-intent scenarios. In view of this, we propose Semantic Frame-Level Multi-Task Self-Consistency (SFL-MTSC), a novel structured aggregation framework operating at the semantic frame level. Instead of output-level majority voting, SFL-MTSC decomposes predictions into intent-specific frames, applies domain--intent grouping and slot-level clustering, and evaluates cluster reliability using path support scoring. Reliable frames are retained and re-integrated to form the final prediction. Zero-shot experiments on the MAC-SLU benchmark dataset show improved slot F1 and overall accuracy over single-path inference, while intent accuracy remains largely stable across most settings.
2606.25552.paper_summary_zh: 基於提示的口語語言理解（SLU）與大型語言模型（LLMs）常常因解碼隨機性而遭遇不一致的意圖-槽結構問題，特別是在多意圖場景中。鑑於此，我們提出了語義框架級多任務自我一致性（SFL-MTSC），這是一種在語義框架級別運作的新型結構聚合框架。SFL-MTSC並不是進行輸出級別的多數投票，而是將預測分解為特定意圖的框架，應用領域-意圖分組和槽級聚類，並使用路徑支持得分來評估聚類的可靠性。可靠的框架被保留並重新整合，以形成最終預測。在MAC-SLU基準數據集上的零樣本實驗顯示，相較於單路徑推理，槽F1和整體準確率有所提高，而意圖準確率在大多數設置中保持相對穩定。
2606.25552.author: Po-Yen Chen et.al.
